title: Darklake
summary: Darklake is a privacy-first Solana DEX and zkAMM stack focused on blocking MEV by hiding trade intent before execution, with Arcium extending that model into encrypted coordination and post-trade privacy.
description:
Darklake positions itself as privacy-forward zero-knowledge infrastructure on Solana, with a launch zkAMM that encrypts trade size, direction, and slippage to block front-running and sandwich attacks. The docs describe a blind-slippage AMM and launch pools starting with USDC-SOL and USDT-SOL.

Arcium and Darklake have both published partnership material describing the combined stack as encrypted execution. Darklake handles pre-trade privacy with zero-knowledge proofs, while Arcium adds MPC-backed private coordination, shared state, dark pools, and post-trade confidentiality.

bodySections.title: Protocol_Surface
bodySections.body: Darklake's docs describe a privacy-first DEX that uses zero-knowledge proofs to keep trade size, direction, and slippage private from submission through execution. The launch docs position the product around a blind-slippage AMM and initial pools for USDC-SOL and USDT-SOL.

bodySections.title: Arcium_Relevance
bodySections.body: This is a confirmed integration with public material on both sides. Arcium's partnership article explains how Darklake's zkAMM covers the pre-trade layer while Arcium powers multi-party coordination and post-trade privacy. Darklake's own blog describes the same relationship as encrypted execution on Solana.

statusNote: Arcium and Darklake both published partnership material describing Darklake's zkAMM plus Arcium MPC as the execution and coordination layer for encrypted DeFi.

sources.label: Darklake Website
